title: libc_setenv/libc_unsetenv duplicated across two crates with no sync enforcement
description: Scenario: wolftpm/src/device.rs and wolftpm-tss/src/connection.rs each contain identical libc_setenv/libc_unsetenv implementations. A comment says 'fix it in both files' but nothing enforces this — no test, no shared module. If a soundness bug is found in one copy (e.g. wrong SAFETY conditions, POSIX edge case), a developer fixing it in one crate will plausibly miss the other. Maintenance consequence: silent divergence between two copies of safety-critical unsafe code that manipulates process-global state. Fix: extract to a small shared internal crate (e.g. wolftpm-internal) or duplicate the fix with a CI check that asserts the two copies are byte-for-byte identical.
notes: No simple mechanical fix. Options: (a) create wolftpm-internal crate with shared setenv helpers, (b) add a CI check comparing the two copies byte-for-byte. Both require broader structural decisions about the crate graph. Defer to pre-1.0 API cleanup pass.
